commentary_text: The sportsbooks price Brazil to win at 58%, nearly 20 percentage points higher than the model's 38%. Both sources agree Brazil is favored, but the market has substantially sharpened that conviction while also compressing the draw probability down to 25% from the model's 32%. The gap on Morocco is similarly wide: the model sees a 30% win probability versus the market's 17%. Recent form, lineup changes, or injury status could all explain why real-money flows are backing Brazil more decisively than the statistical model suggests; the model has no access to such information. The divergence signals a meaningful disagreement worth tracking, though it reflects different information sets rather than offering a judgment on which view is correct.
